understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate the player character to the goal gem tile marked '+' by moving horizontally and using gravity to fall to lower sections of the level, while avoiding spike tiles. The level required careful pathfinding through a multi-floor grid structure.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: MOVE_LEFT and MOVE_RIGHT shift the player one column horizontally, then gravity pulls the player in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid tile. In this level gravity was DOWN (toward higher y values). CLICK removes breakable 'x' tiles to open fall paths - importantly, CLICK does not consume a movement step in the same way moves do. The left_landing and right_landing previews show where the player will end up after a move plus gravity settling. Passage columns (where y changes significantly) indicate ways to reach new floor sections. The level appeared to require navigating down through multiple floors by clicking open breakable blocks and then moving through the resulting gaps. The attempt ended after 50 turns without reaching the goal, suggesting I was too slow or took inefficient paths - spending moves bouncing back and forth rather than committing to a direct downward path toward the goal.
